title: The Japanese Diet approves the law creating a 'sub-capital' in Osaka for emergencies
summary: The law, promoted by Prime Minister Takaiichi and the Ishin party, was approved by a narrow margin in the Diet, generating reactions from regions such as Aichi and criticism about its necessity and transparency.

title: Trump imposes new tariffs on Japan and Australia denounces the measure as unjustified
summary: The US government applied an additional 12.5% tariff on Japanese products alleging forced labor practices, which was rejected by Tokyo and Canberra.

title: Vice president of the Fukuoka Assembly resigns amid allegations of irregularities
summary: Nakao Shuji, who denied the accusations, submitted his resignation considering that no one believed in his innocence, causing surprise in the chamber.

title: The Giants defeat the Tigers and tie for the lead in the Central Baseball League
summary: A two-run home run in the tenth inning by Oyama gave the Giants the victory, tying at the top with the Hanshin Tigers.

title: The extraordinary session of the Diet closes with the approval of 64 laws and criticism over lack of debate
summary: The 221st session closed after approving all government projects, but the opposition and analysts denounced that the prime minister avoided appearances and limited political dialogue.

title: At least 10 dead in a missile attack near Kiev during a defense fair
summary: The attack occurred on the outskirts of the Ukrainian capital while a weapons exhibition was taking place, on the eve of a meeting between US and Ukrainian leaders.

title: Fraud at Prudential Life adds losses of 3.9 billion yen with new cases discovered
summary: 125 new victims and an additional 790 million yen were identified in the scheme of misappropriation of funds by agents of the insurer.

title: The EU rules that TikTok violates the Digital Services Act for negligence in protecting minors
summary: The European Commission issued a preliminary opinion that considers that the Chinese platform does not guarantee the safety of young users, including the prevention of harassment.

title: Trump threatens a massive attack on Iran and says that 'the decision is imminent'
summary: The US president stated that Iran has not suffered enough and that he is prepared for the biggest offensive, in a context of tensions in the Middle East.

title: Court orders SK Group chairman to pay 105 billion yen in South Korea's biggest divorce
summary: The 'divorce of the century' ruling includes the division of conglomerate shares, setting a precedent in South Korean jurisprudence.

title: Anseikon defeats Kirishima and remains alone at the top of the Nagoya sumo tournament
summary: The Mongolian wrestler won the match between the two undefeated, leaving Kirishima with practically no chance of being promoted to yokozuna.

title: Spain declares emergency around Madrid due to an uncontrolled wildfire
summary: High temperatures and drought cause multiple fires in Western Europe, and Spain requests support from the European Union.

title: The price of rice in Japan falls for the fourth consecutive week while stocks exceed adequate levels
summary: 5 kg of rice costs on average 3,373 yen, and private reserves are far higher than necessary, which could continue to put downward pressure on prices.

title: Logistics company Nichirei resumes its operations after a cyberattack that affected its system
summary: A hacker group claimed responsibility for the attack that temporarily paralyzed shipments, while the incident is under investigation.
